steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of the three angles in any triangle is always 180°. Since we know ∠X = 24° and ∠Y = 98°, we can write:

$$∠X + ∠Y + ∠Z = 180°$$

steps.title: Substitute known values and solve for ∠Z
steps.content:
Substitute the known angle measures:

$$24° + 98° + ∠Z = 180°$$

Combine the known angles:

$$122° + ∠Z = 180°$$

Subtract 122° from both sides:

$$∠Z = 180° − 122° = 58°$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
